div1.entryFree:
Fantastic, 1) imaginary, existing only in imagination: by thinking on f. summer's heat, R2 I, 3, 299.
2) indulging the vagaries of imagination, capricious: soothing the humour of f. wits, Ven. 850. to be f. may become a youth, Gentl. II, 7, 47. f. tricks, Meas. II, 2, 121. f. garlands, Hml. IV, 7, 169.
3) incredible, prodigious: who hath done today mad and f. execution, Troil. V, 5, 38.
